---
title: "Day 2 - Block 3"
subtitle: "Literature Review and Research"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D2 B3"
---

## Objective

- Build retrieval workflow and claim-evidence ledger
- Improve source triage and provenance
- Produce one traceable synthesis artifact

## Inputs

- One focused research question
- 4-6 candidate sources
- Claim-evidence ledger template

## Retrieval workflow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  Q[Research question] --> R[Retrieve]
  R --> T[Triage]
  T --> L[Claim-evidence ledger]
  L --> S[Synthesis paragraph]
```

## RAG framing for literature work

- Retrieval-augmented generation reduces unsupported claims
- Use external source snippets for every factual statement
- Keep exact quote and source id in ledger

## Provenance fields (minimum)

- Source title and id
- Exact supporting sentence
- Why the sentence supports the claim
- Uncertainty note if evidence is weak

## Hands-on

- Build ledger with 3 claim rows
- Draft one paragraph from ledger only
- Add one uncertainty/disclosure line

## Deliverable

- One evidence paragraph with provenance
- One claim-evidence ledger (3 rows)
- One verification + disclosure line

## Timebox

- 20 min lecture input
- 30 min group build
- 10 min debrief

## Submission link

- `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=MICRO_D2B3`

## Evaluation rubric

- Claims grounded in cited evidence
- Provenance trace is reproducible
- Uncertainty stated concretely

## Extension task (optional)

- Add one contradictory source
- Revise claim after contradiction

## Resource anchors

- R017, R022, R035
- R055, R063, R064

## Debrief

- Which claim had weakest evidence?
- What would falsify the paragraph?
- Which step should remain human-owned?
